content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : Advice File: Section 82016                    Date : August 1, 1984

                                                        NO. M-84-257


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Carla Wardlow

Subject : Question from Senator Roberti


QUESTION

Senators Roberti and Carpenter at one time jointly controlled
a legislative caucus committee. Control of the committee was
later turned over to Senator Carpenter exclusively. Ultimately,
Senator Roberti was asked to simply sign off on all checks of
the committee of $1,000 or more. Senator Roberti has no
involvement in making decisions about who receives contributions.
The reason he signs the checks is to keep him informed of the
contributions that are being made. He has no discretion with
regard to whether to sign a check, but routinely signs them all.
Should Senator Roberti be listed on the Statement of Organization
as a controlling candidate?

ANSWER

Per Barbara, signature on committee checks alone would not
constitute control unless Senator Roberti has some other sig-
nificant influence on the committee's activities. (This advice
might be different if no other candidate were listed as con-
trolling the committee.)

sections.question:
Senators Roberti and Carpenter at one time jointly controlled 
a legislative caucus committee. 
Control of the committee was 
later turned over to Senator Carpenter exclusively. 
Ultimately, 
Senator Roberti was asked to simply sign off on all checks of 
the committee of $1,000 or more. 
Senator Roberti has no 
involvement in making decisions about who receives contributions. 
The reason he signs the checks is to keep him informed of the 
contributions that are being made. 
He has no discretion with 
regard to whether to sign a check, but routinely signs them all. 
Should Senator Roberti be listed on the Statement of Organization 
as a controlling candidate? 
ANSWER 
Per Barbara, signature on committee checks alone would not 
constitute control unless Senator Roberti has some other sig-
nificant influence on the committee's activities. 
(This advice 
might be different if no other candidate were listed as con-
trolling the committee.)
sections.conclusion: Per Barbara, signature on committee checks alone would not constitute control unless Senator Roberti has some other significant influence on the committee's activities. (This advice might be different if no other candidate were listed as controlling the committee.)
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Senator Roberti, who signs committee checks of $1,000 or more but has no discretion in the decision and no involvement in contribution decisions, should be listed as a controlling candidate on the Statement of Organization.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Signing checks alone does not constitute control under campaign finance law unless the signatory has other significant influence on committee activities, though the analysis might differ if no other candidate were listed as controlling the committee.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear QUESTION and ANSWER sections. Document references 'Barbara' (likely Barbara Kimsey, FPPC staff member) and includes caveat about different outcome if no other controlling candidate exists.
